package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for strings_contains_vs_index: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `strings.Index(s, sub) != -1` or `strings.Index(s, sub) >= 0` patterns
type CaseStringsContainsVsIndexDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseStringsContainsVsIndexModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseStringsContainsVsIndexRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseStringsContainsVsIndexModel
}

type CaseStringsContainsVsIndexService struct {
    repo *CaseStringsContainsVsIndexRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseStringsContainsVsIndexAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeStringsContainsVsIndex(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseStringsContainsVsIndexDTO) error {
    focus := "strings_contains_vs_index"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    _ = strings.Contains(input, ":")
    strings_contains_vs_index_negative := map[string]int{"strings": 1, "contains": 2, "vs": 3, "index": 4}
    strings_contains_vs_index_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    strings_negative_0 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = strings_negative_0
    contains_negative_1 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = contains_negative_1
    vs_negative_2 := strings_contains_vs_index_negative["vs"] + len(input)
    _ = vs_negative_2
    index_negative_3 := strings_contains_vs_index_negative["index"] + len(input)
    _ = index_negative_3
    return nil
}
